title: Original RCT critical-appraisal worksheet (CC-BY, not derived from CASP/JBI)
context: CASP, JBI, SIGN, and AMSTAR appraisal checklists are under non-commercial or share-alike licences incompatible with the project's CC-BY-4.0 output. The project must author original worksheets from methodological first principles, citing established frameworks (EQUATOR/Cochrane/GRADE) as provenance for concepts without reproducing their copyrighted expression. The RCT worksheet is the first, anchoring the M0 slice, and will be used in the annotated walkthrough (task 008).
objective: Author an original, CC-BY-licensed RCT critical-appraisal worksheet from first principles — covering randomisation, allocation concealment, blinding, endpoints, effect size, attrition, and COI/funding — without copying or adapting any non-commercial or share-alike checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: Authored entirely from methodological first principles; cites EQUATOR/CONSORT, Cochrane Handbook, and GRADE as provenance anchors for concepts but copies no third-party checklist text or structure; passes the licence gate clean.

Covers all key appraisal domains: randomisation method and adequacy, allocation concealment, blinding (who and how), primary and secondary endpoints, effect size and precision (including how to read a hazard ratio and confidence interval), attrition and ITT analysis, conflict of interest and funding disclosure.
Written in plain language for a lay audience; questions are framed to guide appraisal, not to render verdicts on specific studies.
Printable and usable offline; format is clean Markdown renderable to PDF.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice.
Every methodological concept cited to a sources.yaml entry with compatible allowedUse; passes the licence + provenance build gate (task 004).
Signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger): clinical framing is correct and nothing reads as treatment guidance.
Signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger): worksheet is usable, plain-language, and does not stray into advice.
Signed off by a methodology reviewer: randomisation, blinding, effect-size, and attrition questions are methodologically accurate.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Scope — original critical-appraisal worksheets; § Data, licensing & compliance — CASP/JBI licence incompatibility)
https://www.equator-network.org/reporting-guidelines/consort/ (CONSORT — cited as methodological authority, not reproduced)
https://training.cochrane.org/handbook (Cochrane Handbook — authority for randomisation/blinding concepts)
output: An original RCT critical-appraisal worksheet (content/worksheets/rct-appraisal.md) committed under CC-BY-4.0, covering all key appraisal domains, provenance-cited, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off plus methodology review.
